comments.body:
> comparePassword is defined after mongoose.model() is called — at that point the schema is already compiled and instance methods won't be attached. Move UserSchema.methods.comparePassword to before the mongoose.model(...) call. (Same bug was fixed in PR #37.) Response format inconsistency — login/refresh error responses use { error: '...' } instead of the project-wide { success, message, data } format. Please align with the convention used everywhere else. catch blocks don't call next(err) — errors in login and refresh are caught and returned directly as 500, bypassing the global errorHandler. This means errors won't be logged. Change catch (err) { return res.status(500)... } to catch (err) { next(err); } and add next to the function signature. GET /:userId in users.js has no try/catch — any database error will be an unhandled rejection. Please wrap the handler in try/catch and call next(err).

--- Fixed, models\User.js moved UserSchema.methods.comparePassword before the mongoose.model, routes\user.js api\user\me → added next to signature and replaced res.status(500) with next(err) ; api\users\:userId → wrapped in try/catch and added next(err)

reviews.body:
comparePassword is defined after mongoose.model() is called — at that point the schema is already compiled and instance methods won't be attached. Move UserSchema.methods.comparePassword to before the mongoose.model(...) call. (Same bug was fixed in PR #37.)
Response format inconsistency — login/refresh error responses use { error: '...' } instead of the project-wide { success, message, data } format. Please align with the convention used everywhere else.
catch blocks don't call next(err) — errors in login and refresh are caught and returned directly as 500, bypassing the global errorHandler. This means errors won't be logged. Change catch (err) { return res.status(500)... } to catch (err) { next(err); } and add next to the function signature.
GET /:userId in users.js has no try/catch — any database error will be an unhandled rejection. Please wrap the handler in try/catch and call next(err).
